title: Webhooks, Replay Defense, SSRF, Unsafe Consumption, and Business Abuse · Compiler Case: Webhook Signature Freshness
summary: A gateway parses before signature verification, accepts stale duplicates, follows arbitrary callback URLs, trusts upstream JSON, and protects expensive workflows only with request counts. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts webhook signature freshness increment in signed ingress, bounded egress, and abuse decision model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Verify provider identity, algorithm, exact bytes, timestamp freshness, constant-behavior signature comparison, key rotation, and failure disclosure before parsing.
Deploy CSP, frame-ancestors, HSTS at the correct authority, MIME sniffing prevention, referrer policy, permissions policy, and cache controls with browser tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Webhook Signature Freshness · Theory Webhook Signature Freshness
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 6112 in a canary revision draining long-lived response streams. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine signed ingress freshness, replay defense, and SSRF egress policy; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then emit a server error and retain one low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
